criteria.label: Run JS/TS with Node and read process.env / process.argv.
criteria.example:
```js
// node script.js Alice 30
const [, , name, age] = process.argv
console.log(`Hello ${name}, age=${age}`)
console.log('key:', process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY)
```
Run TS via `tsx script.ts` or `ts-node script.ts`.

criteria.label: Use package.json scripts and npm/npx to manage and run a project.
Choose and use ESM vs CommonJS consistently.
criteria.example: Set `"type": "module"` in `package.json` → use `import`/`export`. Without it, Node defaults to CommonJS → use `require`/`module.exports`. Pick one for the project and stick to it.

criteria.label: Explain the event loop and why CPU-bound work must be offloaded.
criteria.example: Node runs your JS on a single thread. A `for (let i=0;i<1e9;i++){}` synchronously freezes every request handler for seconds. Offload such work to `worker_threads`, a queue, or a separate service so the event loop stays responsive.
